title: Phish Residency at Sphere - 2026
tagline: Nine night. Zero Repeats. Reinventing how Sphere is used forever.

body: Two years after our first run, Phish returned to Sphere for something far bigger: nine shows over three weekends with not one song repeated — 161 unique songs, more than 27 hours of music.

Any jam could stretch from four minutes to thirty, so the visuals had to follow the band live, in the moment. The 2024 bar had to be cleared by an order of magnitude — every song its own world, reacting in real time, flawless across nine sold-out nights on the largest high-resolution screen on the planet.

I led the project end to end as creative and delivery lead — accountable for the show that hit the screen every night. I directed a global team of 50+ artists across multiple studios, steering 70+ visual content pieces from concept through onsite execution, and owned the creative direction, real-time development, and live show direction across all nine nights.

The centerpiece was a virtual light rig: a real-time engine that recreated a physical light show on Sphere's 160,000 sq ft media plane, with 7,000+ virtual DMX lights played live by the band's longtime lighting designer. Around it we built real-time worlds — iridescent spiderwebs, bubblegum universes, a hot-dog spaceship odyssey — each adapting to the band's setlist in the moment, anchored by high-resolution capture of The Barn, Phish's Vermont studio.
led: 01 — Creative and delivery lead, accountable for the entire show
02 — Directed 50+ artists across multiple studios and 70+ content pieces
03 — Designed and built the real-time virtual light rig and live engine
04 — Led onsite innovation and show direction across nine nights

impact.note: GQ said we "reinvented Sphere forever" — nine sold-out nights, 161 songs without a single repeat, and over 27 hours of music. Another world-first for live entertainment.
